question.text: When the strike price increases (all else constant), the impact on option values is:

answer.text: Call value decreases, Put value increases
Both call and put values increase
Call value increases, Put value decreases

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Higher strike makes it harder for a call buyer to profit (must rise higher) → call value decreases. Higher strike makes it easier for a put buyer to profit (sell at higher price) → put value increases. **The Trap:** The effect is opposite — what benefits puts hurts calls when strike changes. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII covers strike price impact identically. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** For hedging purchases, choose strikes based on your risk budget — lower strikes (ITM calls) cost more but provide better protection; higher strikes (OTM calls) are cheaper but protect less.
